source: Kuailepu manual search first pass from China-reachable network
scope: Western-related candidate pool only. This is not a full inventory of all Kuailepu songs.
nextStep: Foreign-VPN screening closed out the first pass. China-network second-pass discovery found Home on the Range as the next western public-domain candidate to screen under a foreign VPN; Salut d'Amour was found on Kuailepu but cannot enter the current queue because the page only exposes instrument=none.
seedQueries: Angels We Have Heard on High
My Bonnie Lies Over the Ocean
Drink to Me Only with Thine Eyes

foreignVpnScreening.conclusion: This first-pass screened set does not produce a clean next import candidate under the current public-domain-first strategy.

duplicateAliasFinds.notes: Useful discovery for future manual search, but not a new melody page.

chinaSecondPassDiscovery.conclusion: The strongest newly discovered candidate is Home on the Range; most other recognizable hits were already public, duplicate-melody aliases, or lacked usable public instruments.

candidates.searchResultTitle: 爱的礼赞 爱德华 埃尔加
candidates.notes: Recognizable Elgar public-domain title, but this Kuailepu page exposes only instrument=none, so it cannot become a public fingering page under the current product rules.

candidates.searchResultTitle: Vientos Suaves Vientos Suaves
candidates.notes: US-side western searches do not surface a clear canonical tune identity; results skew toward generic ambient/music pages. Hold until a stronger title match or composer identity is found.

candidates.searchResultTitle: Polska Polska
candidates.notes: Western results treat 'polska' mainly as a dance genre rather than one specific melody, so it is too ambiguous to prioritize as a standalone SEO page.

candidates.searchResultTitle: Lullaby of the Manifold 万物摇篮曲

candidates.searchResultTitle: The Last Waltz The Last Waltz
candidates.notes: Western results are dominated by the well-known modern pop song title, which is useful as demand reference but not aligned with the current public-domain import strategy.

candidates.notes: Strong western demand signal across tin whistle / recorder tutorials, but the tune is a modern copyrighted standard rather than a clean public-domain import target.

candidates.searchResultTitle: O son do ar O son do ar
candidates.notes: Composer field is Bieito Romero. Not a public-domain expansion target.
